title: Aloo Gobi

ingredients.name: potato, peeled and cut into 2 cm cubes

subs.name: raw banana (kachha kela) cut into similar cubes for a heavier, more filling version
broccoli florets — blanch for 1 minute first so they don't overcook
1 tsp each of freshly grated ginger and garlic, pound roughly together

ingredients.name: amchur (dry mango powder)
visual_checks: adds a mild tang at the end
ingredients.notes: adds a mild tang at the end

subs.name: a squeeze of lemon juice added at the very end

steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wide kadai over medium-high flame. Add the potato cubes and fry, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes until the edges turn golden. Remove and set aside — they will finish cooking later with the masala.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp oil to the kadai. Splutter the cumin seeds, then add the onion and fry until softened and lightly golden, about 4 minutes.

Add the ginger-garlic paste and green chilli; cook until the raw smell goes, about 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and cook on medium heat until they break down and oil begins to separate, about 5 minutes.

Lower the flame and add turmeric, coriander powder and red chilli powder. Stir for 30 seconds. Return the par-fried potatoes and add the cauliflower florets; toss everything to coat evenly in the masala.

Sprinkle 3 tbsp water over the vegetables, cover with a lid and cook on low flame for 12–15 minutes until the potatoes and cauliflower are fork-tender. Lift the lid and stir every 5 minutes; add a little more water if the masala sticks.

Uncover, increase to medium heat and cook 2–3 minutes to drive off excess moisture and lightly caramelise the edges. Add garam masala and amchur, toss once, taste for salt and finish with fresh coriander. Serve with roti or paratha.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cauliflower → broccoli florets — blanch for 1 minute first so they don't overcook; amchur → a squeeze of lemon juice added at the very end; ginger-garlic-paste → 1 tsp each of freshly grated ginger and garlic, pound roughly together; potato → raw banana (kachha kela) cut into similar cubes for a heavier, more filling version
